Alabama · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Alabama Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology under the ABESPA. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$150
$100 application + $50 license fee
Renewal
$100
Every 2 years
Processing Time
4 to 6 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Alabama Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology

Mailing Address

P.O. Box 304760, Montgomery, AL 36130-4760

Physical Address

100 N. Union St., Suite 724, Montgomery, AL 36130

Phone & Fax

Phone: (334) 269-1434

Fax: (334) 834-9618

Email

abespa@abespa.org

Credential Categories

Alabama SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Alabama. Requires a master's degree, clinical fellowship, and passing Praxis score.

Provisional SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. Allows supervised practice while fulfilling post-graduate experience requirements.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Alabama Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's or doctoral degree in speech-language pathology from a CAA-accredited program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331). The current passing score is 162.

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete a minimum of 36 weeks of full-time supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours) under an ASHA-certified SLP.

Submit Application to ABESPA

Submit the application with the $100 fee plus $50 license fee. Include official transcripts, Praxis scores, CF verification, and background check.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.
  • Background checkCriminal background check as required by ABESPA.
Telepractice

Alabama Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Alabama permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. The compact allows SLPs licensed in member states to practice across state lines through a compact privilege. Alabama-licensed SLPs must comply with state telehealth regulations.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Alabama issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. This allows supervised practice while fulfilling the 36-week post-graduate experience requirement.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Alabama is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact, which allows SLPs licensed in other compact member states to obtain a compact privilege to practice in Alabama. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available for non-compact applicants.

Frequently Asked Questions

Alabama SLP FAQ

How much does an Alabama SLP license cost?

The total initial cost is approximately $150, including a $100 application fee and $50 license fee. Biennial renewal is $100.

Is Alabama part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes. Alabama has enacted ASLP-IC legislation. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Alabama through a compact privilege once the system is fully operational.

Does Alabama allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. Alabama permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Alabama license or ASLP compact privilege.
